title: draba pilosa var. commutata
intro: Draba pilosa var. commutata, also known as hairy whitlowgrass, is a plant species within the mustard family, characterized by its small, white flowers and hairy leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Draba pilosa var. commutata include its potential to aid in the healing of cuts and scrapes, reduce inflammation, and combat bacterial infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or salves to utilize its medicinal properties.
